Nishant Dougall

Northcote , 3070 | 0412202666

nishantdougall@gmail.com

I am a diploma of community services graduate with a business degree, a master's in finance, eight years of project experience in the corporate sector, and four years of community services experience. 

I have volunteered at Thorne Harbour Health for two years and understand the importance of creating a safe, welcoming, client-centred, strengths-based environment for clients of the Positive Living Centre.

As a classic Capricorn, I am hardworking, rational, grounded, and constructive. I am queer, neuro-diverse, gender diverse, culturally diverse, and I have lived experience of psychosocial disability, stigmatization, shame, discrimination, intersectionality, and the social determinants of health.

I am passionate about social justice and eager to work for organizations that bring about meaningful change.

EXPERIENCE

Markets Assistant	May 2023 - Dec 2023

DVCS Markets, Greensborough, VIC

Assisted the Market Leader to manage 65 market days, 80,000 visitors, and 6500 stall bookings across Banyule & Nillumbik in 2023

Oversaw stallholder setup & ensured the site was clean & accessible, which required me to manage several concurrent tasks and perform well under pressure.

Answer queries from stallholders and respond to incidents and potential incidents

Community Support Worker	Nov 2022 - Present

Diamond Valley Community Support, Greensborough, VIC

Providing emergency aid to individuals in crisis, such as vouchers for food, fuel, phone credit, and advocacy. 

Using a client-centered, strength-based approach to service delivery, empowering them to make informed decisions. 

Delivering friendly, non-judgmental, empathetic, and compassionate support while prioritizing client privacy and dignity. 

Assessing and evaluating clients' needs and developing an appropriate support response, including internal and external referral pathways if/when appropriate. 

To date, I have conducted over 400 interviews.

Rainbow Connect COVID19 Food Delivery	May 2020 - Dec 2020

Thorne Harbour Health, South Yarra, VIC

I delivered fresh and frozen meals to socially isolated and immunocompromised LGBTIQ+ clients who survived the HIV epidemic and were now facing the pandemic.

I effectively managed client visits by phoning them in advance to confirm delivery arrangements and interacting with them upon delivery.

Our team delivered over 2000 packages to Positive Living Centre clients during the pandemic.

Sex On Premise Venue (SOPV) Outreach Worker 	Dec 2018 - Dec 2019

Thorne Harbour Health, Melbourne, VIC

I supported clients at SOPVs, allowing them to ask questions about sexual health, HIV, & other health topics & find connections to relevant health services.

Demonstrated sensitivity to the issues of people living with HIV, injecting drug users and other men who have sex with men

Re-Wired v2.0 Peer Support Group Facilitator 	Aug 2018 - Jan 2019

Thorne Harbour Health, St Kilda, VIC

SMART Recovery fortnightly meeting for clients who have completed the Re-Wired eight-week therapeutic program

I used my lived experience to help clients learn skills and strategies to change their methamphetamine use

Topics covered include lapses & relapses, sleep, nutrition, HIV, sex, mental health & mindfulness

Senior Business Analyst	Jan 2016 - Mar 2019

Coutts & Co, London, VIC

As the lead analyst, I was responsible for initiating and gathering requirements for this compliance project.

I successfully established effective collaborations with stakeholders and external organizations professionally.

Business Analyst	Jan 2014 - Dec 2015

Independent Television News (ITN), London, VIC

I was the lead analyst on a tech project in ITN's Finance dept. 

Helped the Project Manager implement new software. 

I created & managed staff training & project comms.

Project Analyst	Dec 2010 - Dec 2013

National Australia Bank (NAB), Melbourne, VIC

I identified and documented requirements for a new centralised process on a significant transformational change program.

I was responsible for designing the solution, drafting the appropriate documents and record keeping as per NAB's policies and procedures regarding project delivery

Business Banker	Jan 2009 - Nov 2010

National Australia Bank (NAB), Melbourne, VIC

I was placed into this role via NAB's Graduate Program, which started with 3000 applicants & established 18 successful graduates in positions throughout the bank.

I supported my manager in looking after a portfolio of 100 small business clients in the Carlton, most with approximately $ 2 million in borrowings.

Melbourne Polytechnic, Preston, VIC

Masters - Finance	Jan 2006 - Dec 2008

Monash University, Melbourne, VIC

Queensland University of Technology, Brisbane, QLD
